seoTitle: Create a Standalone Landing Page on Your Wix Site
metaDescription: Learn how to add an independent landing page to an existing Wix website by hiding the global header and footer for campaigns.
intro: Sometimes you need a page focused entirely on a single campaign or offer without your site's usual navigation distracting visitors. This guide covers adding a new page to an existing Wix site, hiding the global header and footer to isolate it, and building out conversion-focused elements like forms and call-to-action buttons.

steps.heading: Open your site's page directory
steps.body: In the Wix editor, open the Pages and Menu panel to insert a new page.

steps.heading: Add a fresh page
steps.body: Create a new blank page layer that will become your standalone landing page.

steps.heading: Hide the global header and footer
steps.body: Use Page Settings to disable your site's standard header and footer so the page feels isolated from the rest of your navigation.

steps.heading: Set a custom URL and hide it from menus
steps.body: Rename the page's URL slug and disable its default menu visibility so it functions as a standalone destination.

steps.heading: Add conversion elements
steps.body: Drag in visual strips, a lead generation form, and clear call-to-action buttons designed to capture visitor interest.

steps.heading: Check mobile view and preview
steps.body: Toggle mobile view to verify responsive scaling, then preview the full interaction before publishing.

faq.question: Will visitors see my site's regular navigation menu on this page?
faq.answer: No, if you hide the global header and footer in Page Settings, the landing page will display without your site's usual menu.

faq.question: Can I set a custom URL for the landing page?
faq.answer: Yes, you can rename the page's URL slug independently to match your campaign name or promotional link.

faq.question: Should I add SEO metadata to a landing page?
faq.answer: Yes, setting distinct SEO metadata for the page helps you track and index the campaign separately from the rest of your site.

faq.question: Can I reuse this landing page for multiple campaigns?
faq.answer: You can duplicate the page structure for future campaigns, though each one should typically have its own dedicated URL.
